content:
# Registration Renewal Eligibility

This document describes the online vehicle registration renewal flow for a transport customer portal. The goal is to keep the customer journey simple while making eligibility, payment, audit, and notification steps explicit.

## Sequence Diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor Customer
    participant Portal as Customer Portal
    participant IdP as Identity Provider
    participant API as Registration API
    participant Registry as Vehicle Registry
    participant Pay as Payment Gateway
    participant Notify as Notification Service

    Customer->>Portal: Start renewal
    Portal->>IdP: Confirm session
    IdP-->>Portal: Authenticated user
    Portal->>API: Request renewal options
    API->>Registry: Check vehicle record
    Registry-->>API: Status and owner match
    API-->>Portal: Eligibility summary
    Portal->>Customer: Show fee and actions
    Customer->>Portal: Confirm payment
    Portal->>Pay: Authorise payment
    Pay-->>Portal: Payment approved
    Portal->>API: Complete renewal
    API->>Registry: Update expiry date
    API->>Notify: Send receipt
    Notify-->>Customer: Email confirmation
```

## Eligibility Decision Table

| Check | Pass condition | Fail action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Identity | Signed-in user matches record | Ask user to re-authenticate |
| Vehicle status | Registration can be renewed | Show contact/support pathway |
| Inspection | Inspection is current when required | Explain inspection requirement |
| Infringements | No blocking payment holds | Link to fine payment support |
| Payment | Gateway confirms authorisation | Keep renewal pending and allow retry |

## Failure States

| State | User message | Frontend handling |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `AUTH_EXPIRED` | Your session has expired. | Preserve form state and redirect to sign in. |
| `NOT_ELIGIBLE` | This vehicle cannot be renewed online. | Show reason and support contact. |
| `PAYMENT_DECLINED` | Payment was declined. | Allow retry without duplicate renewal. |

## Frontend Implementation Notes

- Use server-state fetching for the eligibility summary and invalidate it after payment.
- Do not optimistically mark a renewal complete before the API confirms the registry update.
- Keep payment and registry writes idempotent with a server-generated transaction id.
- Store audit events server-side; do not log raw identity details in the browser.

## Accessibility and Security Notes

- Error summaries should move focus to the first blocking item.
- Payment errors need plain-language recovery steps.
- OIDC tokens remain server-side or in secure session storage; never expose API keys to the client.
